_doc:
Phase 12 demo — Import UX + Fidelity Mode.

What Phase 12 ships:
  • IMPORT .V button in the bottom toolbar (next to VERILOG export).
  • Drag-and-drop a `.v` file onto the canvas → opens the Import Modal.
  • Modal shows: filename + size, top-module picker (auto-pre-selects `top` if present, last module otherwise), Fidelity-mode toggle (CANONICAL / FIDELITY), the first parse error with line:col + caret + source snippet, an import report (`Imported N modules, X gates, Y flip-flops, …`).
  • Two commit actions: REPLACE CURRENT (whole-scene swap with an undo checkpoint) or ADD AS SUB-CIRCUIT (synthesise a SUB_CIRCUIT node holding the imported scene).
  • Fidelity Mode: re-emits each IR node's `originalText` (captured by elaborate from the AST source range) verbatim instead of pretty-printing from IR. Comments, attributes, unusual whitespace, identifier casing — all survive byte-for-byte.
  • Verilog Block hashing: two users importing the same fragment produce the same canonical hash (whitespace/comments don't affect it; semantics do).

To try the round-trip:
  1. Click IMPORT .V → pick `examples/hdl-corpus/phase12-fidelity-demo.v`.
  2. Toggle FIDELITY mode in the dropdown — see import report stays the same.
  3. ADD AS SUB-CIRCUIT — sample_alu lands on the canvas as a single SUB_CIRCUIT block.
  4. Click VERILOG to re-export. With Fidelity Mode the output preserves `(* keep *)`, the parameter, and the `$display` line verbatim. Canonical mode emits clean IR-driven Verilog.

Verification: `node examples/tests/test-hdl-fidelity.mjs` (round-trip on the sidecar) + `node examples/tests/test-hdl-import-ux.mjs` (helpers, 24 checks).
